hero.heading: I find what's holding your system back – before it holds you back.
hero.subtitle: I work with companies whose system landscape has grown beyond clarity. I identify root causes. And I make sure everything rests on a solid foundation again – regardless of any single person, technology, or discipline.
observations.heading: Does any of this sound familiar?
observations.subtitle: Patterns I see in companies time and again.
observations.1.title: Onboarding
observations.1.reality: Team members who need weeks or months just to understand how the system works.
observations.2.title: Collaboration
observations.2.reality: People and teams talking past each other because everyone has their own way of doing things.
observations.3.title: Quality assurance
observations.3.reality: Conventions and standards that only survive as long as someone is watching.
observations.4.title: System development
observations.4.reality: Systems that get extended instead of structured – even though every change carries risk.
observations.5.title: Knowledge transfer
observations.5.reality: Knowledge that lives in people's heads – and walks out the door when they leave.
observations.6.title: Change management
observations.6.reality: Systems that have outgrown their design – to the point where no one fully understands the dependencies anymore.
principles.heading: Five principles
principles.1.title: Curiosity
principles.1.description: Exploring without assumptions to see what is actually there.
principles.2.title: Understanding
principles.2.description: Grasping how the moving parts interlock.
principles.3.title: Reflection
principles.3.description: Challenging findings until the full picture holds.
principles.4.title: Foresight
principles.4.description: Anticipating what a system needs to withstand tomorrow.
principles.5.title: Essence
principles.5.description: Stripping away what does not serve the solution.
principles.6.title: Endurance
principles.6.description: Delivering results people can rely on.
services.heading: How I support you
services.subtitle: Three paths, depending on where you are.
services.diagnosis.title: Diagnosis
services.diagnosis.subtitle: Straight to the root cause
services.diagnosis.description: You want certainty about what is really going on in your systems and tooling. I work through it layer by layer until the full picture is clear.
services.diagnosis.deliverable: Concrete evidence from your system. No guesswork.
services.guidance.title: Guidance
services.guidance.subtitle: From root cause to solution
services.guidance.description: The root cause is known — or we find it together. Now you want a solution that is built to hold — and someone who guides its implementation.
services.guidance.deliverable: What I build is built to last — beyond the project, beyond individual people and technologies.
services.sparring.title: Sparring
services.sparring.subtitle: Alignment that grows with your system
services.sparring.description: You want someone who knows the full context and stays oriented.
services.sparring.deliverable: Every decision is evaluated in the context of your entire system — not in isolation.

casestudy.subtitle: Anonymized insights from completed projects.

about.heading: Philipp Höllinger
about.text.1: Philipp Höllinger from Salzburg. I anchor decisions in structure — across software, tools, and organizations. I have an eye for the things that go unnoticed. The details that shape the future of a system.
about.text.2: My career began in logistics — coordinating processes between goods, people, and procedures. From there, I moved into software development and finally into architecture: automotive industry, public sector, software consulting.
about.text.3: What I observed along the way: the more people, tools, and AI come into play, the more important it becomes that decisions once made actually last.
about.text.4: I establish the structure that ensures exactly that.
about.text.5: That is why I founded Curfee.
calltoaction.heading: Let's talk about what you're dealing with.
calltoaction.subtitle: No strings attached. Confidential. To the point.
about.who.heading: Systems Architect · Austria
about.who.1: What drives me is an obsession with the things that go unnoticed. The small details that shape the future of systems.
about.who.2: My career started in logistics: coordinating workflows between goods, people, and processes. What stayed with me: when different people with different approaches need to work together, you need a structure with clear guidelines so that processes run consistently and people can rely on them.
about.who.3: After my computer science degree, I moved into software engineering and eventually into architecture, spanning automotive, public sector, and software consulting. Architecture was the natural next step. I always wanted to see the bigger picture because it shapes the details. Understanding both sides, top down and bottom up, to find the best solution. That perspective showed me something I could not let go of: the reliability that was second nature in logistics is the biggest opportunity in the digital world, and at the same time, the thing that is missing most.
about.who.4: The more tools, processes, and people interlock, the more critical it becomes that decisions live in the structure itself. Yet that rarely happens. Structure often emerges not by design but by accident, because no one ever planned for guidelines that enforce themselves within the system. Dedicated roles that own this responsibility are rare. Instead, committed people step in and take it on alongside their actual work, without the backing or the time to make it sustainable.
about.who.5: That is why I founded Curfee: to help companies anchor decisions so they do not depend on specific individuals, particular technologies, or sheer discipline. But on the structure itself.
about.done.heading: What I've done
about.done.example.1.heading: Justice
about.done.example.1.text: A booking system that didn't prevent errors — it enabled them. No transactional integrity, no validation rules, no database constraints. Rooms were double-booked, bookings in the past were possible, conflicting bookings went unchecked. The root cause wasn't poor development — it was the absence of structure that would have prevented these errors by design. I put that structure in place. Duplicate entries disappeared, performance improved by 20% — and for the first time, people could rely on a booked room actually being available for them.
about.done.example.2.heading: Public Administration
about.done.example.2.text: The EU Single Digital Gateway required administrative information to be available in a structured, multilingual, and machine-readable format — in German and English for EU citizens, crawlable for EU infrastructure. I designed a system that lets editors create and update content, tag it with metadata, and publish it automatically. A structured translation workflow was integrated — the first application of its kind in the region.
about.done.example.3.heading: Tourism & Leisure
about.done.example.3.text: For seven years, one student per region spent three months each summer reading emails and manually entering events. Five regions, five students, every year. The root cause wasn't a staffing problem. There was no standardized input format, no shared foundation. I designed a form that event organizers fill out themselves, an automated import that loads the data daily, and an approval mechanism for review and editing. What used to be a summer job is now a click.
about.stand.heading: What I stand for
about.stand.1.title: Root cause over symptom
about.stand.1.description: Real solutions start with understanding what is actually going on.
about.stand.2.title: Structure over discipline
about.stand.2.description: What holds must be embedded in the system, not in individual behavior.
about.stand.3.title: Lasting over workaround
about.stand.3.description: What I build outlasts people and technologies.
about.work.heading: How I work
about.work.1: I don't deliver quick answers — I deliver the right one.
about.work.2: I approach every system without assumptions about where the root cause lies — I know the problem, but not where its root cause lies. Whether it's a software architecture, a tool landscape, a process, or an organizational structure — I always start high-level: what interacts with the problem, what connects to what, what influences what. Only when the full picture is clear do I go deeper.
about.work.3: That also means: I look at more than seems necessary at first glance. Because root causes rarely sit where the symptom is visible.
about.work.4: Once the root cause is found, I strip everything back to what matters. What lies between symptom and root cause and contributes nothing to the solution falls away. What remains gets embedded in a structure that anchors decisions, dependencies, and connections — and holds independently of me.
about.work.5: Depending on the problem, I work alone or involve the team. For diagnosis, I decide based on the situation. For guidance, the team is always part of the process — because I don't implement; I design the structure and guide the execution.
about.vision.heading: What is possible
about.vision.subtitle: When structure takes hold, everything changes.
observations.1.potential: Team members who hit the ground running – because the system shows them how things work.
observations.2.potential: People and teams moving faster – because the system replaces the need to coordinate.
observations.3.potential: Conventions and standards enforced by the system itself.
observations.4.potential: Systems where every improvement builds on the last. And lays the groundwork for the next.
observations.5.potential: Decisions that stand – no matter who is still around.
observations.6.potential: Changes made with confidence. Because the system has defined the dependencies.
services.diagnosis.introduction: You have something specific on your mind and want certainty. I work through your system until the full picture is clear. After that, you have what you need — grounded in concrete evidence from your system.
services.guidance.introduction: The root cause is known, or we find it together. I design the solution and guide the implementation. The implementation itself is done by those with the expertise in the relevant systems, tools, or processes. I make sure the structure holds.
services.sparring.introduction: Your system evolves, and with it the decisions that shape its future. You want someone who knows your system and understands the context. I evaluate decisions, provide honest feedback, and make sure the structural direction holds.
faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Your question isn't here? Bring it to the first conversation.
faq.1.question: How does the first conversation work?
faq.1.answer: No pressure. You tell me what's on your mind, I listen. After that, we both know whether I can help and how.
faq.2.question: Do you implement yourself?
faq.2.answer: No. I design the solution and guide your team through the execution. I leave the implementation to those who have the expertise in the relevant systems, tools, or processes.
faq.3.question: What happens after the diagnosis?
faq.3.answer: You decide. The Diagnosis stands on its own — you can take it from there. If you want, I can take it further with Guidance.
faq.4.question: Do you work remotely or on-site?
faq.4.answer: Both — depends on the scenario. Most of the work happens remotely. On-site when the situation requires it.
faq.5.question: Can I combine services?
faq.5.answer: Yes. A typical path starts with Diagnosis, continues into Guidance, and evolves into Sparring. Each service stands on its own, but they build on each other naturally.
faq.6.question: What exactly do you embed into the structure?
faq.6.answer: Standards, guidelines, and conventions — embedded directly into the structure across software, tools, and organizations, so they apply to everyone and everything automatically, without relying on individual discipline. They hold regardless of who comes and goes.
faq.7.question: How long does a typical engagement take?
faq.7.answer: It depends on the scope of the systems and structures involved. We define it together in the first conversation.
faq.8.question: Do you work within agile frameworks?
faq.8.answer: No. My work requires concentrated, uninterrupted analysis and space for deep thinking. I adapt to your team's rhythm, but the analysis itself needs focused depth.
faq.9.question: Who is this relevant for?
faq.9.answer: Anyone working with software architectures, system and tool landscapes, or processes that have grown organically — and who wants clarity on dependencies, connections, and the anchoring of decisions.
consent.message: This website uses cookies for usage analysis.
cookies.heading: Cookie Policy
cookies.what.heading: What are cookies
cookies.what: Cookies are small text files that are stored on the user's device when visiting a website. They serve to make websites more user-friendly and efficient. Cookies fall into two categories: first-party cookies, which are set by the website being visited, and third-party cookies, which are set by external services integrated into the website.
cookies.legal.heading: Legal basis
cookies.legal: The use of cookies is governed by § 165(3) of the Austrian Telecommunications Act 2021 (TKG 2021), which implements Art. 5(3) of the ePrivacy Directive. Strictly necessary cookies are set on the basis of the legitimate interests of the data controller pursuant to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR, as they are required for the proper operation of the website. Non-essential cookies are only set with the explicit consent of the user pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR.
cookies.types.heading: Types of cookies used
cookies.types.necessary.label: Necessary cookies
cookies.types.necessary: These cookies are required for the basic functionality and security of the website. They are set without consent. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR.
cookies.types.analytics.label: Analytics cookies (Google Analytics)
cookies.types.analytics: These cookies are used to measure website usage and to improve the website based on usage data. They are only set with the explicit consent of the user. Legal basis: Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR. Provider: Google Ireland Limited, Gordon House, Barrow Street, Dublin 4, Ireland. Cookie storage period in browser: 2 years. Data retention: 14 months. Further details are provided in the privacy policy.
cookies.list.heading: Cookie list
cookies.list.name: Name
cookies.list.gastar.name: _ga_*
cookies.manage.heading: Managing cookies
cookies.manage: Cookies can be managed and deleted at any time via the browser settings. Consent to non-essential cookies can be withdrawn at any time. Instructions for managing cookies can be found in the help sections of the respective browser.
cookies.updates.heading: Updates to this policy
cookies.updates: This cookie policy may be updated to reflect changes in the cookies used or for legal reasons. The date of the last update is shown at the top of this page.
privacy.cookies.reference: Details on the cookies used on this website are provided in the cookie policy.
privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.phone.label: Phone
privacy.scope: This privacy policy informs users of this website, in accordance with Art. 13 of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), about the nature, scope, and purpose of the processing of personal data by the data controller.
privacy.controller.heading: Data controller
privacy.personal.heading: Processing of personal data
privacy.personal: Personal data is collected on this website only to the extent that is technically necessary. Processing is subject to purpose limitation and data minimisation. Collected data is under no circumstances disclosed to third parties without a legal basis. The processing of personal data is based on the following legal grounds: Consent pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR — the data subject has given consent to the processing of their personal data for one or more specific purposes. Performance of a contract pursuant to Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR — processing is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the data subject is party, or in order to take steps at the request of the data subject prior to entering into a contract. Legitimate interests pursuant to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR — processing is necessary for the purposes of the legitimate interests pursued by the controller or by a third party, except where such interests are overridden by the interests or fundamental rights and freedoms of the data subject.
privacy.rights.heading: Rights of data subjects
privacy.rights: As a data subject, you have the following rights: Right of access (Art. 15 GDPR) — You have the right to obtain confirmation as to whether personal data is being processed, as well as information about such data and its processing. Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR) — You have the right to request the rectification of inaccurate or incomplete personal data. Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR) — You have the right to request the erasure of your personal data, subject to the conditions laid down in Art. 17 GDPR. Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR) — You have the right to request the restriction of processing of your personal data. Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR) — You have the right to receive your personal data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format. Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR) — You have the right to object at any time, on grounds relating to your particular situation, to the processing of personal data concerning you.
privacy.withdrawal.heading: Right to withdraw consent
privacy.withdrawal: Pursuant to Art. 7(3) GDPR, you have the right to withdraw consent at any time. The lawfulness of processing carried out on the basis of consent before its withdrawal is not affected. Withdrawal can be declared by email.
privacy.complaint.heading: Right to lodge a complaint
privacy.complaint: If you believe that the processing of your personal data violates the GDPR, you have the right, pursuant to Art. 77 GDPR, to lodge a complaint with the competent supervisory authority.
privacy.ssl.heading: SSL/TLS encryption
privacy.ssl: For security reasons and to protect the transmission of personal data, this website uses SSL or TLS encryption. An encrypted connection can be identified by the padlock icon in the address bar and by the address beginning with "https://".
privacy.cookies.heading: Cookies
privacy.links.heading: Links to third-party websites
privacy.links: This website contains links to third-party websites. When accessing these external links, data may be transmitted to the respective operator. The controller accepts no liability for the content and data processing by third parties. Users are encouraged to review the privacy policies of linked websites.
privacy.hosting.heading: Hosting
privacy.hosting: This website is hosted on GitHub Pages, a service provided by GitHub, Inc., 88 Colin P Kelly Jr St, San Francisco, CA 94107, USA. When accessing this website, information is automatically recorded in so-called server log files. This includes: IP address, date and time of access, requested resources, browser and operating system used. This data is stored temporarily for security purposes and deleted after a short period. Processing is based on the legitimate interest pursuant to Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR to ensure the uninterrupted operation of the website. For the transfer of personal data to the USA (third country), GitHub employs standard contractual clauses pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR. GitHub is also certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
privacy.analytics.heading: Google Analytics
privacy.analytics: This website uses Google Analytics, a web analytics service provided by Google Ireland Limited, Gordon House, Barrow Street, Dublin 4, Ireland. Google Analytics is activated exclusively with the consent of the user pursuant to Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR. Consent preferences are stored for 30 days. The following data is collected: pages visited and timestamps, interactions on the website, traffic source, device and browser information, geographic information (country/region), IP address (anonymized). Data is retained for 14 months. Google employs standard contractual clauses pursuant to Art. 46(2)(c) GDPR and is certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
privacy.analytics.more: Further information on how Google Analytics handles user data can be found in Google's privacy policy.

services.ideal.heading: Your situation
services.differentiator.heading: The standard
services.diagnosis.ideal.1: You are working with a systems and tooling landscape that has grown organically — and you no longer know where the real risks and dependencies lie.
services.diagnosis.ideal.2: Any change — to tools, processes, or systems — has unforeseen consequences, because the dependencies and connections are no longer traceable.
services.diagnosis.ideal.3: You want confidence before making decisions — based on concrete evidence, not gut feeling.
services.diagnosis.differentiator: Each insight points to a precise location within your system. The path from symptom to root cause is traceable step by step and can be independently verified. The results are ready to act on.
services.guidance.ideal.1: The root cause is known, or you want to find it together and resolve it directly.
services.guidance.ideal.2: Guidelines and standards exist — but are not followed, because there is no structure to anchor them. It should not be discipline that ensures compliance, but the structure itself.
services.guidance.ideal.3: You want solutions that survive the next staff turnover, tool migration, or reorganization — not just a quick fix that treats the symptom.
services.guidance.differentiator: Standards, conventions, and guidelines — embedded in the structure — that apply to every person and every tool. Each decision builds on the previous one and strengthens the foundation for the next.
services.sparring.ideal.1: Your systems are evolving — and with them the decisions that determine whether dependencies and connections remain manageable.
services.sparring.ideal.2: Teams and departments are building their own tooling and workflows. You want someone who ensures the shared direction holds — before the system landscape diverges.
services.sparring.ideal.3: You value well-founded feedback that considers the entire system.
services.sparring.differentiator: My feedback always considers the full context of your system — not just the decision at hand, but how it aligns with the standards, conventions, and guidelines already in place.
